seo_description: Current time in Montevideo, Uruguay. Uruguay Time (UYT, UTC-3) year-round — no DST since 2015. Live clock and time differences for Montevideo.

faq.question: What time is it in Montevideo right now?
faq.answer: Montevideo, Uruguay uses Uruguay Time (UYT, UTC-3) year-round. Uruguay abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2015. The IANA identifier is America/Montevideo. Montevideo is on the same time offset as Buenos Aires (ART, UTC-3) and São Paulo, Brazil (BRT, UTC-3) year-round. The live clock above shows the current Montevideo time.

faq.question: What time zone is Montevideo in?
faq.answer: Montevideo, Uruguay uses Uruguay Time (UYT, UTC-3) year-round. The IANA identifier is America/Montevideo. Uruguay abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2015. Montevideo is on the same UTC-3 offset as Buenos Aires (Argentina) and the main cities of Brazil.

faq.question: Does Montevideo observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Montevideo, Uruguay abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2015. Uruguay now uses Uruguay Time (UYT, UTC-3) permanently year-round. Uruguay previously observed DST (UYST, UTC-2) but stopped due to energy savings evidence being inconclusive.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Montevideo and London?
faq.answer: Montevideo (UYT, UTC-3) is 3 hours behind London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer time (BST, UTC+1), Montevideo is 4 hours behind London. Uruguay does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Montevideo and New York?
faq.answer: Montevideo (UYT, UTC-3) is 2 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Montevideo is 1 hour ahead of New York. Uruguay does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Montevideo?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Montevideo are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM UYT, Monday through Friday. Montevideo is Uruguay's capital and home to about half the country's population. Uruguay has the highest Human Development Index in Latin America and a strong financial services sector. Montevideo hosts several regional headquarters for Latin American operations. The Montevideo Stock Exchange (BVM) and banking sector follow standard UYT business hours.
